metadata.seed: I was already having a really bad day, from a run in I had with a very rude lady. Then I received a text from someone who I thought was my friend. She felt like I haven' t been there for her because I didn't pick up her call. I honestly didn't see her call and definitely would've got back to her, if I saw her call. She said mean and nasty things about me, which I don' t care about. But she did the wrong mistake of bringing my child into the equation, when he had nothing to do with it. I wanted to really beat the crap out of her because not only did she do that, she blocked my number and ran. Like I was beyond pissed.

patient.query: I was already having a really bad day, from a run in I had with a very rude lady. Then I received a text from someone who I thought was my friend. She felt like I haven' t been there for her because I didn't pick up her call. I honestly didn't see her call and definitely would've got back to her, if I saw her call. She said mean and nasty things about me, which I don' t care about. But she did the wrong mistake of bringing my child into the equation, when he had nothing to do with it. I wanted to really beat the crap out of her because not only did she do that, she blocked my number and ran. Like I was beyond pissed.

triggers: run in with a rude lady
text from someone I thought was my friend
friend felt I haven't been there for her
mean and nasty things said about me
my child being brought into the equation
automatic_thoughts: I would have got back to her if I saw her call
I wanted to beat the crap out of her

neutral: Worried about being a bad mother (finding)

llm_response.response:
FINAL RESPONSE:
"It sounds like that interaction really ignited a lot of frustration for you, especially with the added layer of the friend bringing your child into it. When you mentioned wanting to 'beat the crap out of her,' I wonder how that feeling of being completely overwhelmed might have shaped your reaction to the whole situation."

patient.query: Yeah, I guess it did. It just felt like she crossed a line, you know? I was already feeling so stressed and then to have that on top of it... I don't usually get that angry, but everything just built up. I felt so trapped, and I didn't know what to do. It's like I can't handle any more chaos right now.

automatic_thoughts: I can't handle any more chaos

llm_response.response: It sounds like the situation added another layer of stress to what you were already carrying, and that anger you felt probably came from feeling overwhelmed. It makes sense that you'd feel trapped when things feel like they're building up beyond your capacity to cope.

patient.query: Yeah, exactly. It’s like I was holding everything in for so long, and then just one little thing tipped me over the edge. I hate feeling that mad, especially with someone I care about. I just wish I could have handled it differently. The guilt just makes it worse.

triggers: one little thing tipping over the edge
automatic_thoughts: I wish I could have handled it differently
the guilt just makes it worse

llm_response.response: It sounds like you've been carrying a lot of pressure inside, and that small moment just felt like it reached its breaking point. Anger and guilt can really weigh on you, especially when it involves someone you care about.

patient.query: Yeah, it really does. I hate feeling like I’m always on this edge, ready to snap. I want my friendships to feel easy, you know? But lately, it just feels like I'm always second-guessing everything. What if I push them away by being honest about how I feel?

automatic_thoughts: I might push them away by being honest

neutral: Anxiety about saying the wrong thing (finding)
Fear of saying the wrong thing (finding)

llm_response.response:
FINAL RESPONSE:
"It sounds really difficult to navigate these friendships, especially when you're worried about saying the wrong thing. When you mentioned being honest might push people away, what makes you think that could happen?"

patient.query: I guess I just remember times when I tried to be honest before, and it didn’t go well. Like, I’d share something that was bothering me, and it ended up making things worse. I worry that if I’m too open, they’ll think I'm needy or dramatic. It’s hard to shake that feeling, like I'm just a burden.

triggers: times when I tried to be honest
sharing something that was bothering me
automatic_thoughts: if I’m too open, they’ll think I'm needy or dramatic

entailment: Fear of saying the wrong thing (finding)
Fear of being a burden to others (finding)
neutral: Anxiety about saying the wrong thing (finding)

llm_response.response: It sounds like those past experiences have created a strong association for you between being open and negative outcomes. Can you tell me more about what happened in those situations? What did you imagine would be the consequences?

patient.query: Yeah, well, there was this one time when I tried to talk about something that upset me. I thought we had a good friendship, but when I opened up, I just got silence. It felt like I was just... talking to a wall. I ended up feeling so embarrassed, like I had ruined everything. I guess I imagined they'd get annoyed or see me as too much to handle, and that just makes me want to keep everything inside.

triggers: trying to talk about something that upset me
automatic_thoughts: I thought we had a good friendship
I imagined they'd see me as too much to handle

entailment: Fear of saying the wrong thing (finding)
neutral: Anxiety about saying the wrong thing (finding)

llm_response.response: "It sounds like that experience left you feeling really alone, as if no one was there to listen. When you imagine others might react negatively, it can feel overwhelming. Can you tell me more about how that moment made you feel afterward?"

patient.query: Yeah, it was pretty isolating. I felt so rejected, like I had put myself out there for nothing. The silence just echoed in my head afterward, and I kept replaying it, wondering if I had said the wrong thing. It made me doubt myself, like maybe I shouldn’t have even tried to reach out. I felt really small and just... ashamed.

automatic_thoughts: I put myself out there for nothing
I might have said the wrong thing
I shouldn't have tried to reach out

llm_response.response: It sounds like that experience left you feeling really alone and misunderstood, and the shame you're carrying is heavy. It's completely understandable how that interaction could make you question yourself and feel like you shouldn't have reached out at all.

patient.query: Yeah, that pretty much sums it up. It’s just hard to shake off that feeling of being misunderstood. I want to be authentic, but then I feel like I can't trust how people will react. It’s like I’m stuck between wanting to connect and wanting to protect myself from getting hurt again. It’s exhausting.

automatic_thoughts: I can't trust how people will react

llm_response.response: It sounds like those conflicting desires are weighing heavily on you. When you mentioned feeling exhausted, what did that exhaustion feel like in your body?

patient.query: Yeah, it really does weigh me down. The exhaustion feels heavy, like my chest is tight, and it's hard to breathe sometimes. I get this ache in my shoulders too, like I’m carrying all this weight. I just feel drained all the time, and it makes me not want to deal with anything, especially friendships. It’s like I’m stuck in this loop, and I just want to break free from it.

behaviors: not wanting to deal with anything

llm_response.response: That sounds incredibly difficult to carry, especially when it feels so heavy on your shoulders. It's understandable that you'd find it hard to engage with friendships when you're feeling this way. Can you tell me more about what makes you feel caught in this loop?

patient.query: Yeah, it’s like every time I think about reaching out, I get this wave of anxiety, and then I just freeze. I start imagining all the ways it could go wrong—the awkward silence, the judgment. So, instead of doing anything, I just pull away. And then I feel lonely, which just makes me more anxious about trying again. It’s this cycle that keeps wrapping around me, making it tough to see a way out.

llm_response.response: That sounds really difficult to manage. When you mentioned imagining the worst possible outcome, what makes you think that would actually happen?
